results.title: Monster Beverage AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Monster Beverage Corporation demonstrates minimal visible AI adoption, with limited evidence of artificial intelligence implementation across its operations. While the energy drink giant has achieved strong financial performance with $2.20 billion in Q3 2025 net sales[1] and continues expanding globally with 43% international revenue[1], the company appears to be in the early stages of digital transformation. The company has adopted enterprise resource planning software and modernized some supply chain operations[2], but lacks the comprehensive AI strategy seen in competitors like PepsiCo or Coca-Cola. Monster's hiring signals show only 4.9% of job postings mentioning AI[3], indicating limited organizational emphasis on artificial intelligence capabilities. The company's focus remains primarily on product innovation and traditional marketing approaches rather than technological differentiation.

Innovation is backed by upgraded analytics for SKU flow, display optimization and cooler resets. In addition, the company has refined its merchandising strategy to focus on high-impact placements across the convenience, mass and grocery channels.
...
What AI initiatives has Monster Beverage implemented?
...
Monster Beverage has limited visible AI implementation, primarily using ERP software for supply chain management and basic analytics for merchandising optimization.
...
Beverage compare to competitors in AI adoption?
...
Monster lags behind competitors like PepsiCo and Coca-Cola, which have comprehensive AI strategies including digital twins, generative AI partnerships, and cloud-based AI platforms.
...
What is Monster Beverage's hiring approach for AI talent?
...
Only 4.9% of Monster's job postings mention AI keywords, indicating minimal organizational focus on building AI capabilities compared to other beverage companies.
...
Does Monster Beverage use AI for product development?
...
No clear evidence of AI use in product development; the company relies on traditional market research and consumer feedback for innovation.
...
The company focuses on supply chain optimization through ERP systems and merchandising analytics, rather than comprehensive AI transformation.

results.title: Phillip Kauffman
highlights:
Phillip Kauffman is a husband, father, and a dedicated professional who thrives on solving complex challenges. With a background in Graphic Design, and diverse industry experience, he has successfully navigated various creative fields from airbrushing custom cars, to designing movie posters, to overseeing creative as the Creative Director at Monster Energy. Phillip's journey eventually led him to his current role as the Sr. Director of Marketing Technology at Monster Energy. In this position, he bridges the gap between Marketing, Creative, and IT, leveraging his expertise to translate business needs into innovative technology and workflow solutions. Outside of his professional pursuits, he seeks flow states in action sports, such as mountain biking and surfing, and has an insatiable wanderlust for international travel. Phillip remains committed to continuous learning; Completing his MBA, and is also dedicated to expanding his linguistic skills by studying Spanish. Design Portfolio:

Over the past five years, we’ve built something powerful - evolving from a few analytics capabilities into a one-stop insights ecosystem. Now we’re making it more unified and intelligent - connecting what happened, to why it happened, to what to do next. Because when Walmart and our supplier partners operate with greater speed, precision, and reliability, customers feel the difference.
...
This evolution is grounded in three priorities:
...
1. Personalization: a seamless, unified experience that brings data, tools and workflows together into a clear, unified view
2. Speed: using AI to surface what needs your attention, now - highlighting opportunities, flagging risks, and guiding your next best step
3. In-store execution: Enabling real-time visibility that helps supplier field teams act faster where it matters most - in our stores
...
This chapter isn’t about more data. It’s about using it better.

results.title: Monster Energy Privacy Counsel | Wfhforgeon
highlights:
Monster Energy Privacy Counsel | Wfhforgeon
...
# Monster Energy Privacy Counsel
...
- Company: Monster Beverage Corporation (NASDAQ: MNST) is the second largest leading producer of energy drinks worldwide, distributing its products in more than 150 countries. As a publicly traded Fortune 500 company with annual revenue exceeding $7 Billion, Monster has a portfolio of well-known brands and collaborates with business partners worldwide. Known for its bright green M Claw icon, and product taglines like “Unleash the Beast!,” Monster has built a global reputation as one of the world’s leading developers, marketers, and distributors of energy drinks. It continues to build on its success; 2023 represented the company’s 31st consecutive year of increased net sales.
...
- Position Overview: Monster Energy is seeking an experienced privacy attorney to join its Legal Department. In this exciting and highly visible role, you will provide subject matter expertise and counseling on a broad range of privacy related requirements and restrictions worldwide, as well as cybersecurity best practices. You will advise multiple internal client groups and support various privacy initiatives, with a focus both on U.S. and global data privacy compliance.
...
- Experience: Five+ years of experience as a practicing attorney, preferably at a law firm, in-house or regulatory agency focused on issues related to privacy, data protection, and information management. Juris doctor degree required.
...
- Location: Corona, CA with a hybrid work schedule of at least 3 days per week on site in the office.
- Relocation: Open to relocation candidates.
- Compensation: Base salary targeted at $180k plus up to 20% bonus
- Bar: Candidate must be a member of the California State Bar or otherwise qualified to practice law in California as registered in-house counsel.

results.title: Jon Otterstedt
highlights:
Oh hey, I'm glad you stopped by, thanks! Since you’re here let me get you some info. I love learning, working on improving myself, and enjoying life so that I can make unique and positive contributions to the organizations that I work for! I consistently strive to do more and challenge the status quo. WHAT I DO I have a background in graphic design. I leverage the creative skills that I gained to deliver innovative solutions that elevate an organization's effectiveness. I first joined Monster Energy as a Senior Graphic Designer, then was promoted to a POS Media and Content Manager. In that role, I collaborated with the UI/UX team to implement applications, workflows, and optimal user experiences. In my most current role as a Senior Content Manager in Sales Operations, I am leading the implementation of a B2B global eCommerce POS online ordering platform (Monster Shop) on SAP Cloud Commerce using SAP metadata to automate processes and enable visibility on order status and inventory across departments. WHAT I HAVE ACHIEVED • I co-developed a project management and job request system to integrate with Open Text Data Asset Management System, creating processes to streamline internal communication and support over 5,000 jobs per year. • I have developed and managed all POS platform content to align with the POS catalog (iPad app) that pushes out to all sales iPad and iPhones. • Set up APIs into DAM to pull created assets into Monster Garage, ASM, Virtual NACS, internal recognition, and training websites to ensure consistent branding.

results.title: Dan Patel
highlights:
As a motivated and dependable data analyst, I possess a well-rounded skill set in manufacturing, data entry, customer service, data visualization, problem solving, and inventory management. I have a passion for working with data and using it to solve complex business problems. My excellent computer literacy and quick study of new technology and software enable me to adapt to different systems and tools with ease. I thrive in fast-paced environments and maintain accuracy and quality while multitasking. My technical skills include expertise in Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Power BI, Access, Teams, Visio, SQL, Tableau, Python, Spark, QuickBooks, Spreadsheets, ERP Systems, data collection, machine learning, Pandas, APIs, JSON, data models, and more. I possess strong verbal and strategic writing skills, as well as the ability to manage teams, improve processes, and think creatively. I have worked on several projects that showcase my skills, including designing tables to hold data from CSV files and importing that data into a SQL database, performing calculations and analyzing financial data using Python, and creating a flask API data set and hosting a website on Amazon Web Services to showcase visuals and data analysis. Currently, I am working as an IT Manager at Apnar Pharma LP, where I am leading a drug traceability project managing cloud-based IT service to monitor and analyze data from complex data sets in order to meet FDA compliance standards. I am also responsible for managing inventory tracking systems, analyzing product return and sales data, providing written reports for products listed for destruction, and utilizing Microsoft Power BI and Excel to provide analysis reports for management and business stakeholders. Prior to this, I worked as a finance clerk, volunteer tax preparer, seasonal sales associate, and manufacturing technician. I hold a Data Analytics Certificate from the University of California, Irvine, and a Registered Income Tax & Small Business Accounting Certificate from Norco College. I also have a BA in Business Economics from the University of California, Riverside, with related coursework in banking analytics, financial accounting and reporting, advanced macroeconomics, advanced microeconomics, land and resource economics, the stock market, statistics for economics, econometrics, and environmental economics.
...

results.title: SRUJAN CHANDRA
highlights:
Data Scientist / AI–ML Engineer | Machine Learning, NLP/LLMs | MLOps (MLflow, Airflow, Docker) | Databricks & AWS
...
I’m a Data Scientist / AI–ML Engineer with hands-on experience building machine learning, NLP, and LLM-driven solutions that make a real impact in production. My work spans the full end-to-end pipeline—cleaning and preparing data, designing features, training and tuning models, deploying them into cloud environments, and maintaining them through strong MLOps practices. I’ve worked on a variety of applied ML use cases, including forecasting, classification, recommendation systems, and text-based applications such as sentiment analysis, summarization, and embedding-powered retrieval. I enjoy working with modern AI frameworks like PyTorch, transformer models, and RAG workflows, especially when turning unstructured text into practical insights and automation. On the engineering side, I regularly use tools such as Databricks, PySpark, Airflow, MLflow, AWS, and Docker to build scalable pipelines and reproducible experiment setups. I like designing ML system architectures, integrating models into APIs, and ensuring reliability through monitoring, versioning, and automated retraining. I value clarity, collaboration, and building solutions that are not only technically sound but genuinely useful for the business. I’m continually exploring new developments in LLMs, retrieval systems, and MLOps—and I enjoy applying these ideas to create AI systems that are efficient, explainable, and built for real-world use.
...
### Data Scientist AI ML Engineer at [PepsiCo](https://www.linkedin.com/company/pepsico) (Current)
...
In my current role as a Data Scientist / AI–ML Engineer at Pepsico, I work across the full lifecycle of machine learning systems—from shaping raw data into usable features to deploying, monitoring, and improving production models. I build reliable end-to-end pipelines using Python, PyTorch, scikit-learn, MLflow, and Airflow, and collaborate closely with engineering and data teams to create scalable ML architectures on AWS and Databricks. My work spans forecasting, classification, recommendation systems, and NLP/LLM applications. I’ve developed text-based solutions such as sentiment analysis, document summarization, and embedding-driven retrieval using transformer models and RAG pipelines. I also focus heavily on MLOps, including experiment tracking, CI/CD for ML workflows, model versioning, drift detection, and automated retraining. Along with delivering production-ready models, I build monitoring and explainability frameworks using SHAP and custom dashboards to ensure fairness, accuracy, and stability. I enjoy prototyping new AI capabilities—especially in the generative and LLM space—and documenting system designs so the solutions we build are reproducible, maintainable, and ready for long-term adoption.
...

results.title: Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI job with VIB | 12860981
highlights:
Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI job with VIB | 12860981
...
# Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI
...
Employer VIB Location Leuven Closing date 21 Aug 2026
...
## Job Details
...
Application deadline: August 31, 2026 About us VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact. We are now looking for a Machine Learning (ML) Engineer to join the Machine Learning Expertise Unit at VIB.AI. Your role As an ML Engineer, you will: Develop and deploy ML solutions in close collaboration with researchers across VIB centers, applying state-of-the-art methods to cutting-edge biomedical questions.Design, implement, and evaluate ML and foundation-model approaches, including LLM-based solutions, for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.Collaborate with researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI can accelerate scientific progress.Work within the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and with VIB AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools.Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of AI in line with best practices and international standards.Leverage VIB Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale ML development and deployment. Your profile PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, statistics, or a related field.Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).Experience developing AI applications for scientific or biomedical research, including LLM-based and agentic AI systems, is a plus.Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.A strong interest in applying ML to impactful life science problems.We welcome applications from both early-career and experienced ML engineers. Our offer A stimulating, international, interdisciplinary, collaborative, and supportive work environment, which emphasizes diversity and inclusion.An environment that promotes personal development.The opportunity to pioneer applications of ML in biomedical sciences.Direct access to VIB’s Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.A competitive salary and benefits package, with relocation support if needed.The chance to make a lasting impact on science, health, and society. About VIB.AI VIB.AI is the artificial intelligence initiative of VIB, designed to accelerate breakthroughs in life sciences by integrating advanced AI methods into research, discovery, and innovation. For more information, visit vib.ai/en. How to apply Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool (https://jobs.vib.be/apply/135914). A complete application file should contain the following documents: Your motivation letter stating career goals, past experience, and how these relate to the current position.Your CV.Contact information of three references. Application deadline: August 31st 2026. For further information, please send an email to pedro.goncalves@kuleuven.be, dmitry.kobak@ugent.be or stein.aerts@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: ML Engineer.

highlights:
# IIT Roorkee Opens Admissions for the 11th Batch of its Post Graduate Certificate in Data Science, Machine Learning & Generative AI

Published: 2026-06-26T09:14:24+00:00
Source: ahmedabadmirror.com (ahmedabadmirror.com)
Language: en

## Story

IIT Roorkee Opens Admissions for the 11th Batch of its Post Graduate Certificate in Data Science, Machine Learning & Generative AI

Roorkee (Uttarakhand) [
